MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JULY 15, 2014 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS UNIVERSITY OF THE PACIFIC AND ARTHUR A DUGONI SCHOOL OF DENTISTRY'S MOTION TO STRIKE LATE-FILED 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND ORDERING PLAINTIFF TO REIMBURSE DEFENDANT FOR ITS REASONABLE ATTYS' FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF FILED ITS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT BEYOND THE 10 DAY PERIOD TO AMEND, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED PREJUDICE BY THE LATE-FILING. NO TRIAL DATE IS SET AND DISCOVERY IS IN ITS EARLY STAGES. FURTHER, ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF ADDED A CAUSE OF ACTION BEYOND THE SCOPE OF THE PERMITTED AMENDMENT, THE COURT DECLINES TO STRIKE THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR JUDICIAL ECONOMY REASONS. IF PLAINTIFF WERE TO FILE A MOTION TO ADD THE CAUSE OF ACTION, IT WOULD ALMOST CERTAINLY BE GRANTED, GIVEN THE CASE IS STILL AT THE EARLY STAGES AND CALIFORNIA'S LIBERAL POLICY REGARDING AMENDMENTS. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' MOVING PAPERS DO NOT ADDRESS WHAT COSTS WILL ACTUALLY BE INCURRED IN FILING AN AMENDED ANSWER AND SUPPLEMENTAL DISCOVERY RESPONSES. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES FOR OPPOSING THIS MOTION IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: GISELLE CASEY, CSR # 8098 (302)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-10-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. PARDEEN TUMBER OF THE LAW OFFICES OF DANIEL FEDER, TEL. NO. 391-9476 BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. CAROLINE M. COLANGELO OF THE DELFINO MADDEN O'MALLEY COYLE & KOEWLER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (916)661-5700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS UNIVERSITY OF THE PACIFIC and ARTHUR A DUGONI SCHOOL OF DENTISTRY'S MOTION TO STRIKE LATE-FILED 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND OR ORDERING PLAINTIFF TO REIMBURSE DEFENDANT FOR ITS REASONABLE ATTYS' FEES AND COSTS is Denied. Although Plaintiff filed its First Amended Complaint beyond the 10 day period to amend, Defendants have not established prejudice by the late-filing. No trial date is set and discovery is in its early stages. Further, although Plaintiff added a cause of action beyond the scope of the permitted amendment, the Court declines to strike the cause of action for judicial economy reasons. If Plaintiff were to file a motion to add the cause of action, it would almost certainly be granted, given the case is still at the early stages and California's liberal policy regarding amendments. Defendants' request for fees and costs is denied. Defendants' moving papers do not address what costs will actually be incurred in filing an amended answer and supplemental discovery responses. Plaintiff's request for fees for opposing this motion is denied. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR # 8098 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2014 Continued To Dec-10-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-06-2014 Continued To Oct-22-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Claire Cochran, Esq. of the Law Offices of Daniel Feder, Tel. 415.391.9476, for plaintiff and opposing party by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The court did not allow argument because Plaintiff's counsel did not give proper notice that she will be appearing to argue or contest the tentative ruling. Therefore, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS DAVID GIRALDO, UNIVERSITY OF THE PACIFIC, ARTHUR A DUGONI SCHOOL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted with leave to amend as to the 4th cause of action for failure to prevent because the retaliation cause of action is currently defective. The motion is denied as to the failure to prevent harassment claim. The Court SIGNED THE ORDER that was submitted via email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Claire Cochran, Esq. of the Law Offices of Daniel Feder, Tel. 415.391.9476, for plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The court did not allow argument because Plaintiff's counsel did not give proper notice that she will be appearing to argue or contest the tentative ruling. Therefore, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS DAVID GIRALDO, UNIVERSITY OF THE PACIFIC, ARTHUR A DUGONI SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The court sustains the demurrer to the 7th cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy without leave to amend. The cause of action is duplicative of the 6th cause of action. The court overrules the demurrer to the 8th cause of action for disability harassment. Personnel decisions can support the claim under Roby v. Mckesson Corp. (2009) 47 Cal.4th 686. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the claim and defendant raises a factual issue. The court sustains the demurrer with leave to amend as to the 5th cause of action for retaliation. Plaintiff fails to allege protected activity per Rope v. Auto-Chlor System of Washington (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 635. Plaintiff merely alleges that he requested a disability accommodation. The motion to strike is granted with leave to amend as to the 4th cause of action for failure to prevent because the retaliation cause of action is currently defective. The motion is denied as to the failure to prevent harassment claim. The Court SIGNED THE ORDER that was submitted by the moving party via email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-15-2014 Continued To Jul-24-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS DAVID GIRALDO AND UNIVERSITY OF THE PACIFIC, SCHOOL OF DENTISTRY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to July 24, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 15, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
